# services.taler

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.taler`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.taler.exchange.debug` | `boolean` | Whether to enable debug logging. |
| `services.taler.exchange.denominationConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | This option configures the cash denomination for the coins that the exchange offers. For more information, consult the [upstream docs](https://docs.taler.net/taler-exchange-manual.html#coins-denomination-keys). You can either write these manually or you can use the `taler-harness deployment gen-coin-config` command to generate it. Warning: Do not modify existing denominations after deployment. Please see the upstream docs for how to safely do that. |
| `services.taler.exchange.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the GNU Taler exchange. |
| `services.taler.exchange.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to open ports in the firewall |
| `services.taler.exchange.package` | `package` | The taler-exchange package to use. |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | Configuration options for the taler exchange config file. For a list of all possible options, please see the man page [`taler-exchange.conf(5)`](https://docs.taler.net/manpages/taler-exchange.conf.5.html) |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings.exchange.CURRENCY` | `non-empty string` | The currency which the exchange will operate with. This cannot be changed later. |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings.exchange.CURRENCY_ROUND_UNIT` | `string` | Smallest amount in this currency that can be transferred using the underlying RTGS. For example: "EUR:0.01" or "JPY:1" |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings.exchange.DB` | `value "postgres" (singular enum)` | Plugin to use for the database. |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings.exchange.MASTER_PUBLIC_KEY` | `string` | Used by the exchange to verify information signed by the offline system. |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings.exchange.PORT` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port on which the HTTP server listens. |
| `services.taler.exchange.settings.exchangedb-postgres.CONFIG` | `non-empty string` | Database connection URI. |
| `services.taler.includes` | `list of absolute path` | Files to include into the config file using Taler's `@inline@` directive. This allows including arbitrary INI files, including imperatively managed ones. |
| `services.taler.merchant.debug` | `boolean` | Whether to enable debug logging. |
| `services.taler.merchant.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the GNU Taler merchant. |
| `services.taler.merchant.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to open ports in the firewall |
| `services.taler.merchant.package` | `package` | The taler-merchant package to use. |
| `services.taler.merchant.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | Configuration options for the taler merchant config file. For a list of all possible options, please see the man page [`taler-merchant.conf(5)`](https://docs.taler.net/manpages/taler-merchant.conf.5.html) |
| `services.taler.merchant.settings.merchant.DB` | `value "postgres" (singular enum)` | Plugin to use for the database. |
| `services.taler.merchant.settings.merchant.LEGAL_PRESERVATION` | `string` | How long to keep data in the database for tax audits after the transaction has completed. |
| `services.taler.merchant.settings.merchant.PORT` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port on which the HTTP server listens. |
| `services.taler.merchant.settings.merchant.SERVE` | `one of "tcp", "unix"` | Whether the HTTP server should listen on a UNIX domain socket ("unix") or on a TCP socket ("tcp"). |
| `services.taler.merchant.settings.merchantdb-postgres.CONFIG` | `non-empty string` | Database connection URI. |
| `services.taler.runtimeDir` | `string` | Runtime directory shared between the taler services. Crypto helpers put their sockets here for instance and the httpd connects to them. |
| `services.taler.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | Global configuration options for the taler config file. For a list of all possible options, please see the man page [`taler.conf(5)`](https://docs.taler.net/manpages/taler.conf.5.html) |
| `services.taler.settings.taler.CURRENCY` | `non-empty string` | The currency which taler services will operate with. This cannot be changed later. |
| `services.taler.settings.taler.CURRENCY_ROUND_UNIT` | `string` | Smallest amount in this currency that can be transferred using the underlying RTGS. You should probably not touch this. |
